fields.description:
## Why

Audit Maintainability finding #1: `reconcile.py:697` (and the reconciler's `_loader` helper) loads sibling modules by file path (`_load("reconcile_run_differs", "run_differs.py")`) instead of normal package imports. This defeats every static tool the repo relies on — Pyright/Serena reference resolution, import-linter layering checks, IDE navigation, and the grounding oracle — in the repo's largest and most change-heavy subsystem (~40 modules, 5 of the 10 biggest files). The audit rated the reconciler the highest-onboarding-cost area partly because of this. The mechanism predates the reconciler shipping as a proper package; it is now pure legacy.

## What

Convert the `_engine/rebar_reconciler` path-based `_load()` calls to ordinary `from . import …` / `import rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler.…` package imports, delete the `_load()` helper once callerless, and let the existing lazy-import pattern (module-level function-local imports with rationale comments) handle any genuine circular-import seams the conversion surfaces.

## Scope

In: all `_load()` call sites under `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/`, the loader helper itself, and any import-cycle untangling the conversion forces. Out: any behavioral change to reconciliation, module splits/renames (janitor epics own refactors — this story changes only *how modules are referenced*, which is why it rides this epic despite the scope boundary; flagged at creation).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Zero path-based `_load(` call sites remain under `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/` (grep-verifiable)
- [ ] The `_load` helper is deleted outright — the file-path loader (`spec_from_file_location`-based `_load()` and the `_loader.py` `lazy_load` bootstrap) is removed once callerless, with no deprecation stub left behind (deletion is final; there is no future migration path to preserve)
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green; reconciler test suites (`tests/scripts/bridge/`, reconciler units) pass unchanged
- [ ] Pyright/Serena can resolve references into the converted modules (spot-check: find-references on a converted symbol returns its call sites)
- [ ] No new import cycles: `python -c "import rebar._engine.rebar_reconciler"` and the optionality guard stay green
